name: Sekmet
shortDescription: Sekmet is the Egyptian goddess of healing, medicine, destruction, and warfare. She is depicted as a lioness-headed woman and embodies both fierce power and protective strength.
description: Sekmet, also spelled Sakhmet, is a powerful and complex figure in Egyptian mythology. She is primarily known as the goddess of war, destruction, plagues, and healing. Depicted as a woman with the head of a lioness, Sekmet embodies both fierce aggression and immense protective power. Her name translates to "The Powerful One" or "The Mighty One," reflecting her formidable nature. In myths, Sekmet is often associated with the scorching heat of the sun and is sometimes considered an aspect of the goddess Hathor, particularly in her wrathful form. One well-known myth recounts how Ra, the sun god, sent Sekmet to punish humanity for their wickedness. Her bloodlust was so intense that Ra had to intervene, tricking her into drinking beer dyed red to resemble blood, which pacified her. As a healing deity, Sekmet was believed to possess the power to cure diseases and ward off plagues. Priests of Sekmet were skilled physicians, and temples dedicated to her often served as centers of medical knowledge. She was considered a protector of pharaohs and a symbol of their military might. The duality of Sekmet's nature – both destructive and healing – highlights the ancient Egyptian understanding of balance and the interconnectedness of opposing forces. She is a crucial figure in the Egyptian pantheon, representing the raw power of the divine and the potential for both devastation and restoration.

sources.title: The Complete Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt
sources.author: Richard H. Wilkinson
